meta.description: A LangGraph-based ReAct agent harness with human-in-the-loop tool approval and a reusable fan-out/fan-in orchestration pattern for parallel research sub-agents.

nodes.label: __start__
nodes.description: Entry point of the compiled LangGraph StateGraph (builder.set_entry_point("assistant")).

nodes.label: assistant
nodes.description: Main ReAct LLM node. Streams a ChatTogether completion bound to ALL_TOOLS, reassembles the streamed chunks into a single AIMessage, and appends it to state.messages.

nodes.label: check_tool_permission
nodes.description: Human-in-the-loop gate. If ask_tool_permission == 'y', calls interrupt() to pause the graph and prompt the user to approve/deny the pending tool call. On denial, injects a ToolMessage rejection. On approval (or when gating is off), passes through unchanged.

nodes.label: tools (ToolNode)
nodes.description: LangGraph prebuilt ToolNode wrapping every entry in TOOL_NODE_ROUTED_TOOLS (all real tools except the virtual fan-out tool). Executes the tool the assistant chose and returns a ToolMessage.

nodes.label: research_agent_node ×N (parallel)
nodes.description: Fan-out worker. One instance is dispatched per query via langgraph.types.Send. Each runs an independent Tavily search, logs the raw response, and synthesizes it with a dedicated research_llm call. Results accumulate into state.research_results via the add_or_reset reducer.

nodes.label: compile_research
nodes.description: Fan-in join point. Joins all research_results into one string, wraps it in a single ToolMessage addressed to the original parallel_internet_research_agent tool call, records it in compiled_results, and resets research_results to [].

nodes.label: __end__
nodes.description: Terminal node reached when the assistant produces a final answer with no further tool calls.

edges.label: invoke
edges.description: Graph entry point routes directly into the assistant node.

edges.label: tool call(s) emitted
edges.description: check_tool_or_end (nodes.py:57-66) sees an AIMessage with tool_calls and routes to the permission gate.

edges.label: no tool calls
edges.description: check_tool_or_end sees a plain AIMessage with no tool_calls and routes to END; this is a final answer.

edges.label: approved / auto-approved
edges.description: after_check_tool_permission (nodes.py:92-106) sees the tool call was approved (or gating disabled) and routes to the ToolNode for a standard tool.

edges.label: rejected → denial ToolMessage
edges.description: User answered anything not starting with 'y' at the interrupt prompt. A ToolMessage denial was appended to state; after_check_tool_permission detects it and loops back to assistant.

edges.label: fan-out (Send ×N)
edges.description: after_check_tool_permission detects the tool name is parallel_internet_research_agent (a VIRTUAL_TOOLS_FAN_OUT member) and returns one Send("research_agent_node", {query: q}) per query — dispatched concurrently.

edges.label: tool result
edges.description: graph.py:35 static edge. The ToolMessage produced by the executed tool is fed back to the assistant to continue reasoning.

edges.label: join
edges.description: graph.py:33 static edge. Every parallel branch converges here once all Send-dispatched workers complete.

edges.label: compiled research ToolMessage
edges.description: graph.py:34 static edge. The synthesized, joined research is handed back to the assistant as a single tool result.

routed.name: internet_research
routed.description: Single web search via TavilyClient; expects a full natural-language query. Logs request/response to search_logs (tagged 'single').

routed.name: list_directory
routed.description: Lists entry names in a directory (defaults to cwd).

routed.name: is_directory
routed.description: Checks whether a path is a directory.

routed.name: is_file
routed.description: Checks whether a path is a regular file.

routed.name: get_absolute_path
routed.description: Resolves a path to its absolute form.

routed.name: list_files_recursive
routed.description: Recursively lists files matching a glob pattern.

routed.name: make_directory
routed.description: Creates a directory tree, including missing parents.

routed.name: get_file_size
routed.description: Returns a file's size in bytes.

routed.name: find_file_in_directory
routed.description: Non-recursive filename search in one directory, with optional fuzzy fallback; handles '*' wildcards by splitting and recursing.

routed.name: find_file_in_directory_or_subdirectories
routed.description: Recursive filename search across an entire directory tree, with optional fuzzy fallback.

routed.name: search_file_contents
routed.description: Case-insensitive keyword/phrase search within a file, returning matches with surrounding context lines.

routed.name: get_file_contents
routed.description: Reads a file's text content, truncated to ~1000 tokens.

routed.name: write_markdown_file
routed.description: Writes markdown content to a timestamped file, creating the directory if needed.

routed.name: get_full_directory_information
routed.description: Returns name, is_file, last_modified, and size for every entry in a directory.

virtual.name: parallel_internet_research_agent
virtual.description: Never executed directly — its no-op body always returns "". Intercepted by after_check_tool_permission to trigger the fan-out pattern via Send, one research_agent_node per query.

fields.name: messages
fields.description: Full conversation history; standard LangGraph message-append reducer.

fields.name: ask_tool_permission
fields.description: Whether check_tool_permission should interrupt for human approval before every tool call.

fields.name: parallel_tool_calls
fields.description: Whether the assistant LLM may emit multiple tool calls in one turn. Kept 'n' in practice — incompatible with per-call approval.

fields.name: research_results
fields.description: Accumulates one synthesized summary per fan-out branch; reset to [] by compile_research once joined.

fields.name: query
fields.description: Per-branch input consumed by a single research_agent_node instance (set via the Send payload).

fields.name: compiled_results
fields.description: Running, indexed history of every compiled research result across the session.
API key for the ChatTogether LLM backend.
API key for the Tavily web search client.
Model identifier passed to both the assistant and research ChatTogether instances.
Max output tokens for LLM completions.
'y' to interrupt for approval before every tool call, 'n' to auto-approve.
'y'/'n'. Validated at startup: cannot be 'y' at the same time as ASK_TOOL_PERMISSIONS='y' (settings.py:21-25).
Directory for search_logs.log JSONL search request/response records.
Directory for response_history.jsonl full conversation dumps.
Directory for general_log.log standard Python logging output.
DEBUG | INFO | WARNING | ERROR | CRITICAL — threshold for general_log.log.

logging_targets.name: search_logs
logging_targets.when: Every web search call, tagged single_or_multiple = 'single' or 'multiple'.
logging_targets.note: research_agent_node wraps the write in a threading.Lock since fan-out branches may write concurrently.

logging_targets.name: agent_response_logs
logging_targets.when: Once, on clean exit ('!exit'). Dumps the full final message history via messages_to_dict.

logging_targets.name: general_logs
logging_targets.when: Continuously, at every node entry (assistant, check_tool_or_end, check_tool_permission, after_check_tool_permission) and on cleanup, at the LOGGING_LEVEL threshold.

flows.name: Startup & Graph Compilation
flows.summary: Process boot: render the architecture diagram if missing, build the system prompt, and initialize a fresh checkpointed thread.
flows.trigger: Process launched via `uv run react-orchestrator` (or `python -m react_orchestrator.main`).

steps.description: If docs/agent_graph.png does not exist, shell out to `uv run python utils/draw_correct_flow.py` to render it via matplotlib.
Module import time: StateGraph is built with all 5 nodes and edges, then builder.compile(checkpointer=MemorySaver()) produces the importable `graph` object.
Build the initial SystemMessage — either the caller-supplied system_prompt or DEFAULT_SYSTEM_MSG (main.py:23-33), which instructs the model on tool usage conventions.
Generate a thread_id (agent_name + timestamp) and wrap it in a RunnableConfig — this is the checkpointer key that gives the session persistent memory.
Construct the initial AgentState with empty messages and ask_tool_permission / parallel_tool_calls pulled from Settings.

flows.name: Standard Conversational Turn (No Tool Use)
flows.summary: The simplest path: the user asks something the model can answer directly, with no tool calls at all.
flows.trigger: User enters a prompt at the REPL that the LLM can answer without any tool.

steps.description: REPL reads the prompt, appends a HumanMessage to agent_state["messages"].
graph.stream(agent_state, config, stream_mode='messages') is called; tokens from the assistant node are printed as they stream.
assistant node streams the ChatTogether completion and reduces the chunks into one AIMessage.
check_tool_or_end finds no tool_calls on the AIMessage and returns END.
The inner permission-check loop finds state.next is empty (no pending interrupt) and breaks immediately — back to the prompt.

flows.name: Tool Call — Auto-Approved
flows.summary: ASK_TOOL_PERMISSIONS='n': any standard tool the model chooses runs immediately, no interrupt.
flows.trigger: Model emits a tool call (e.g. get_file_contents) while ask_tool_permission == 'n'.

steps.description: assistant emits an AIMessage with one tool_call (parallel tool calls are hard-disabled elsewhere, nodes.py:74).
check_tool_or_end routes to check_tool_permission because tool_calls is non-empty.
check_tool_permission sees ask_tool_permission != 'y' and returns {} without calling interrupt().
after_check_tool_permission sees the last message is still the AIMessage (not a ToolMessage) and the tool isn't in VIRTUAL_TOOLS_FAN_OUT, so it routes to "tools".
The prebuilt ToolNode executes the chosen tool function and appends its ToolMessage; the static edge sends control back to assistant.

flows.name: Tool Call — Human Approval Granted
flows.summary: ASK_TOOL_PERMISSIONS='y': the graph pauses on an interrupt() and waits for the user to type 'y' before running the tool.
flows.trigger: Model emits a tool call while ask_tool_permission == 'y', and the user approves.

steps.description: check_tool_permission builds a human-readable prompt ("Call tool: X with arguments: ...") and calls interrupt(prompt) — this suspends the graph run entirely; graph.stream() returns with nothing further to yield from this node.
The REPL's inner loop calls graph.get_state(config); since state.next and state.tasks[0].interrupts are set, it reads the interrupt value and prompts the user directly with input(interrupt_value).
User types 'y' (or anything starting with 'y').
Resuming via Command(resume=user_input) re-enters check_tool_permission; tool_decision[0] == 'y' so it returns {} unchanged — approval granted, message state untouched.
after_check_tool_permission routes to tools as in the auto-approved case; ToolNode executes and control returns to assistant.

flows.name: Tool Call — Human Approval Denied
flows.summary: The user declines the pending tool call; the assistant is told the call was denied and must respond without the tool's output.
flows.trigger: Model emits a tool call while ask_tool_permission == 'y', and the user answers anything not starting with 'y'.

steps.description: interrupt() pauses the graph with the same approval prompt as the approved path.
REPL detects the pending interrupt, prompts the user, and resumes the graph with Command(resume=user_input).

tool_decision.strip().lower()[0] != 'y' — check_tool_permission builds a ToolMessage stating "The user has denied the invocation of the tool {name}", tagged with the original tool_call_id, and returns it as the new last message.
after_check_tool_permission sees the last message is a ToolMessage (the denial) and routes straight back to "assistant" — the tools node is never reached.
assistant re-invokes the LLM with the denial ToolMessage in context, and must respond or try a different approach.

flows.name: Single Web Search (internet_research)
flows.summary: The model chooses a single, non-parallel Tavily search rather than the fan-out research agent.
flows.trigger: Model emits internet_research(query) for a request that doesn't need to be split into independent sub-queries.

steps.description: DEFAULT_SYSTEM_MSG instructs the model to pass the user's full natural-language intent as the query, not a keyword string.
internet_research's docstring reiterates the same constraint directly to the model at tool-selection time.
Routed through the standard ToolNode like any other real tool (subject to the same approval flow as tool_hitl_approved / tool_autoapproved).
TavilyClient.search() runs with search_depth='advanced', max_results=10, exclude_domains=[medium.com, linkedin.com, facebook.com].
Full response is appended to search_logs.log as JSONL tagged single_or_multiple='single'.
Results are flattened to "From {title}:\n{content}" blocks joined by newlines and returned as the tool's string output (becomes the ToolMessage content).

flows.name: Parallel Research Fan-Out / Fan-In
flows.summary: The model splits a research task into N independent queries; each runs concurrently in its own research_agent_node, and the results are synthesized back into one tool result.
flows.trigger: Model emits parallel_internet_research_agent(queries=[...]) for a task with multiple independent sub-questions.

steps.description: parallel_internet_research_agent's real body just returns "" — it exists purely as an LLM-facing schema; it is never actually executed as a tool function.
check_tool_permission still runs the normal approval gate first (if ask_tool_permission == 'y', the user approves/denies the fan-out call as a whole).

after_check_tool_permission checks if the tool name is in {t.name for t in VIRTUAL_TOOLS_FAN_OUT}; if so it reads the `queries` list and returns [Send("research_agent_node", {"query": q}) for q in queries] — LangGraph dispatches these concurrently.
Each research_agent_node instance runs an independent TavilyClient.search() (search_depth='advanced', max_results=5, chunks_per_source=3, exclude_domains as elsewhere).
Writes are appended to search_logs.log tagged 'multiple', guarded by a module-level threading.Lock (_search_log_lock) because branches may write concurrently.

Results are formatted with per-result relevance scores and summarized by a dedicated research_llm.invoke() call using a synthesis-focused system prompt; the summary is returned as {"research_results": [analysis.content]}.
The add_or_reset reducer on AgentState.research_results appends each branch's summary onto the shared list as branches complete.
All branches converge on the static edge research_agent_node → compile_research once every Send-dispatched task finishes.

compile_research joins all research_results with "


", wraps them in one ToolMessage tagged with the original tool_call_id, appends to compiled_results (add_by_increment reducer, state.py:12-17), and resets research_results to [] so the next fan-out starts clean.
compile_research → assistant static edge hands the synthesized research back to the main LLM loop.

flows.name: Multi-Turn Conversation Memory
flows.summary: Follow-up turns reuse the same thread_id, so LangGraph's MemorySaver checkpointer restores full prior context automatically.
flows.trigger: User enters a second (or later) prompt in the same REPL session, after the first turn reached END.

steps.description: memory = MemorySaver(); graph = builder.compile(checkpointer=memory) — every invocation is checkpointed under its thread_id.
The RunnableConfig's configurable.thread_id is generated once at startup and reused for every graph.stream()/graph.get_state() call for the life of the process.
On follow-up turns, start_flag != 0, so the REPL simply appends a new HumanMessage to the in-memory agent_state["messages"] list rather than rebuilding it.

graph.stream(agent_state, config=config, ...) re-enters the graph at "assistant"; because the checkpointer already holds prior state under this thread_id, LangGraph merges/continues rather than starting fresh.
Reaches check_tool_or_end → END exactly as in the first turn if no further tools are needed; the cycle repeats for however many prompts the user enters.

flows.name: Session Termination & Response Logging
flows.summary: On '!exit', the REPL fetches the final checkpointed state and flushes the complete conversation to disk before the process ends.
flows.trigger: User types '!exit' at either the initial or a follow-up prompt.

steps.description: The REPL detects the '!exit' sentinel and `break`s out of the outer while-loop.
final_state = graph.get_state(config) retrieves the last checkpointed AgentState for this thread_id.

cleanup_before_quit(final_state) converts state.values['messages'] via langchain_core.messages.messages_to_dict, wraps it with a timestamp, and appends one JSON line to AGENT_RESPONSE_LOGS/response_history.jsonl.

Cross-cutting: throughout the entire session, every node call has already been emitting logger.debug/info records to GENERAL_LOGS/general_log.log at the configured LOGGING_LEVEL — this is independent of and continues right up to process exit.
